dict.string: Get your local and external IP addresses.

# Get all local IPv4 addresses (only lines containing "broadcast") localipv4_arr=($(ifconfig | grep 'inet ' | grep 'broadcast' | awk '{print $2}')) # Get external IPv4 address from your service externalipv4=$(curl -4 -s -m 5 "$service")
# Get all local IPv6 addresses for interfaces with '%en' localipv6_arr=($(ifconfig | grep 'inet6' | grep '%en' | awk '{print $2}' | sed 's/%en[0-9]*//')) # Get external IPv6 address from your service externalipv6=$(curl -6 -s -m 5 "$service")
subtitletext='Press ↵ to paste or ⌘C to copy'
# If no addresses were found, default to 'n/a' if [ ${#localipv4_arr[@]} -eq 0 ]; then localipv4_arr=("n/a") fi
if [ -z "$externalipv4" ]; then externalipv4="n/a" fi
if [ ${#localipv6_arr[@]} -eq 0 ]; then localipv6_arr=("n/a") fi
if [ -z "$externalipv6" ] || [ "$externalipv6" == "$externalipv4" ]; then externalipv6="n/a" fi
# Build the JSON output. We’ll create an item for each address. json_output="{\"items\": [" first_item=true
# Append each local IPv4 address as a separate item. for ip in "${localipv4_arr[@]}"; do if [ "$first_item" = false ]; then json_output+="," fi json_output+="{\"title\": \"Local IPv4: $ip\", \"subtitle\": \"$subtitletext\", \"arg\": \"$ip\"}" first_item=false done
# Append external IPv4. json_output+=",{\"title\": \"External IPv4: $externalipv4\", \"subtitle\": \"$subtitletext\", \"arg\": \"$externalipv4\"}"
# Append each local IPv6 address. for ip in "${localipv6_arr[@]}"; do json_output+=",{\"title\": \"Local IPv6: $ip\", \"subtitle\": \"$subtitletext\", \"arg\": \"$ip\"}" done
# Append external IPv6. json_output+=",{\"title\": \"External IPv6: $externalipv6\", \"subtitle\": \"$subtitletext\", \"arg\": \"$externalipv6\"}"
json_output+="]}" echo "$json_output"

An Alfred 5 workflow for getting your local and external IP addresses.
[Readme](https://github.com/April-June-August/alfred-ip-address-workflow/blob/master/README.md)
Address of a service to query your external IP from
